subject: Ecology
title: Cloud Forests
description: A short lesson to help you apply Cloud Forests.

hook.text: High in tropical mountains, forests live inside clouds. Mist is their lifeline.

buildup.text: Cloud forests capture moisture directly from fog, supplementing rainfall dramatically.

discovery.text: Trees draped in moss and ferns drip water constantly, feeding streams below.

twist.text: Climate change is pushing clouds higher, drying out forests that depend on persistent mist.

climax.text: Many cloud forest species exist in narrow altitude bands—nowhere else to go if conditions shift.

punchline.text: When the clouds rise, the forest dries.

quiz.question: How do cloud forests get much of their water?
options: By capturing moisture directly from fog
